name: Italy
welcome: Ciao! Welcome to Italy — the home of pizza, pasta, ancient ruins, and gondola rides!
summary: Italy is a beautiful country in southern Europe, shaped like a boot kicking a ball!
It has been home to some of the greatest artists, architects, and scientists in history.
Italy is famous for its delicious food, ancient history, and breathtaking cities.

LOCATION.intro: Italy looks like a boot — let's find it on the map!

items.title: Italy in Europe
narration: Italy is a long peninsula that sticks out into the Mediterranean Sea.
Look at the map — it really does look like a boot about to kick a ball!
The islands of Sicily and Sardinia are also part of Italy.

LANDMARKS.intro: Italy has so many amazing landmarks — it is like living in a museum!

items.title: The Colosseum
narration: The Colosseum is a giant oval stadium built in ancient Rome nearly 2,000 years ago!
Up to 80,000 people would pack inside to watch gladiator contests.
It still stands today — one of the greatest buildings ever made.

items.title: Leaning Tower of Pisa
narration: The Leaning Tower of Pisa really does lean — and nobody planned it that way!
The soft ground caused it to tilt as it was being built 800 years ago.
Today, millions of tourists visit and take funny photos pretending to push it!

items.title: Venice Canals
narration: Venice is a magical city built on top of 118 small islands!
Instead of roads, Venice has canals — so people travel by boat.
The long, elegant boats called gondolas are steered by people with long poles.

FOOD.intro: Italian food is loved by children everywhere in the world!

items.title: Pizza
narration: Pizza was invented in Italy — in the city of Naples!
A true Italian pizza has a thin, crispy base with tomato sauce and fresh mozzarella cheese.
The Margherita pizza uses red tomato, white cheese, and green basil — the colours of Italy's flag!

items.title: Gelato
narration: Gelato is Italian ice cream — but creamier, denser, and more flavourful than regular ice cream!
It comes in dozens of flavours including pistachio, tiramisu, and hazelnut.
On a hot day in Italy, gelato from a little shop is absolutely perfect!

items.title: Pasta
narration: Italy makes over 350 different shapes of pasta!
Spaghetti, penne, rigatoni, farfalle — they all taste different even with the same sauce.
Italians are very serious about pasta — it must be cooked perfectly!

CITIES.intro: Italy's cities are open-air museums of history and beauty!

items.title: Rome
narration: Rome is called the Eternal City — it has been important for over 2,800 years!
Almost everywhere you walk in Rome, you find ancient ruins, fountains, and art.
Throwing a coin in the Trevi Fountain means you will return to Rome one day!

LANGUAGE.intro: Italian sounds like a beautiful song — let's learn some words!

items.title: Ciao — Hello and Goodbye
narration: Ciao is an Italian word that means both hello AND goodbye!
Say it with me — CHOW!
Italians are very expressive — they talk with their hands as well as their words.

FUN_FACTS.intro: Italy is bursting with extraordinary facts!

items.title: A Country Inside Italy
narration: Inside Rome, there is a tiny country called Vatican City!
It is the smallest country in the world — smaller than many city parks.
Vatican City is the home of the Pope and the Catholic Church.

items.title: Leonardo da Vinci
narration: Leonardo da Vinci was born in Italy over 500 years ago — and he was a genius!
He painted the Mona Lisa, designed flying machines, and studied the human body.
He drew designs for helicopters and tanks centuries before they were invented!
